2:34.arabic: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
2:34.english: And when We said to the angels: Prostrate to Adam — they prostrated, except Iblis. He refused and was arrogant, and became of the disbelievers.
2:34.score_notes: Full origin story of Iblis — far more memorable than a trimmed fragment. Story context principle.

lesson_use.heading: 7 · اسْتَكْبَرَ (was arrogant)
lesson_use.arabic: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَ**اسْتَكْبَرَ** وَكَانَ مِنَ الْكَافِرِينَ
lesson_use.english: And when We said to the angels: Prostrate to Ādam — they all prostrated, except Iblīs. He refused and was **arrogant**, and became of the disbelievers
lesson_use.notes: The origin story of arrogance. When the angels were commanded to bow, every single one obeyed — except one. He claimed greatness for himself, and that is what this word means.

2:163.arabic: وَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
2:163.english: Your God is one God — there is no god but He, the Most Merciful, the Continuously Merciful.
2:163.score_notes: Triple إِلَٰه in one verse — the complete tawhid statement. Pattern: إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ + لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا.

lesson_use.heading: 11 · إِلَٰه (god)
lesson_use.arabic: وَ**إِلَٰهُكُمْ** **إِلَٰهٌ** وَاحِدٌ ۖ لَا **إِلَٰهَ** إِلَّا هُوَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ الرَّحِيمُ
lesson_use.english: And your **God** is one **God** — there is no **god** but He, the Most Merciful, the Continuously Merciful
lesson_use.notes: Three times in one ayah — **إِلَٰه** appears three times. This single verse is the Qur'an's most complete statement of tawḥīd.

2:185.arabic: شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَاتٍ مِّنَ الْهُدَى وَالْفُرْقَانِ فَمَن شَهِدَ مِنكُمُ الشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ
2:185.english: The month of Ramadan is that in which the Quran was revealed as guidance for mankind — clear proofs of guidance and the criterion. So whoever among you witnesses the month, let him fast it.
2:185.score_notes: Ramadan verse. Extends shahida — here it means to be present during / to witness (the month), not legal testimony. Short and connected to a practice every Muslim does.

lesson_use.english: Whoever witnesses the month (of Ramadan) should fast it
lesson_use.notes: Ramadan verse. Extends the meaning of shahida — here not legal testimony but to be present during / to witness (the month). Teaches that the same root carries multiple shades of witnessing.

2:219.arabic: يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْخَمْرِ وَٱلْمَيْسِرِ ۖ قُلْ فِيهِمَآ إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنَـٰفِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُمَآ أَكْبَرُ مِن نَّفْعِهِمَا
2:219.english: They ask you about wine and gambling. Say: In them is great sin and benefit for people, but their sin is greater than their benefit.
2:219.score_notes: Famous ruling verse on wine/gambling. Uses both kabir and akbar — two forms in one sentence. Very long.

lesson_use.rejection_reason: Very long. Both forms already covered elsewhere.
